rows.currentQuote: Ye know that after two days is the feast of the passover, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified.
rows.currentText: Foretells his crucifixion at Passover — after two days is the feast, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified

rows.currentQuote: Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good work upon me. For ye have the poor always with you; but me ye have not always. For in that she hath poured this ointment on my body, she did it for my burial. Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, there shall also this, that this woman hath done, be told for a memorial of her.
rows.currentText: Defends the woman who anointed him — she has done a good work, pouring ointment for my burial, and shall be remembered wherever the gospel is preached

rows.currentQuote: Go into the city to such a man, and say unto him, The Master saith, My time is at hand; I will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples.
rows.currentText: Sends two disciples to prepare the Passover meal at the appointed house

rows.currentQuote: Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
rows.currentText: Announces at the Last Supper that one of the Twelve will betray him

rows.currentQuote: He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish, the same shall betray me. The Son of man goeth as it is written of him: but woe unto that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! it had been good for that man if he had not been born. Thou hast said.
rows.currentText: Identifies the betrayer as the one who dips with him — better for that man if he had never been born

rows.currentQuote: Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me. For ye have the poor with you always, and whensoever ye will ye may do them good: but me ye have not always. She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying. Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world, this also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.
rows.currentText: Defends the woman who anointed him — she has prepared his body for burial and will be remembered everywhere

rows.currentQuote: Go ye into the city, and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water: follow him. And wheresoever he shall go in, say ye to the goodman of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples? And he will shew you a large upper room furnished and prepared: there make ready for us.
rows.currentText: Sends two disciples to follow a man with a water jar to the upper room and prepare the Passover

rows.currentQuote: Verily I say unto you, One of you which eateth with me shall betray me.
rows.currentText: Foretells the betrayal at the Passover table — one of those eating with him will hand him over

rows.currentQuote: It is one of the twelve, that dippeth with me in the dish. The Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him: but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! good were it for that man if he had never been born.
rows.currentText: Names the betrayer as one of the Twelve dipping in the dish — better for that man if he had never been born

rows.currentQuote: All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered. But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.
rows.currentText: Foretells the disciples will all fall away tonight — and after he rises he will go before them into Galilee

rows.currentQuote: Verily I say unto thee, That this day, even in this night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.
rows.currentText: Foretells Peter will deny him three times before the rooster crows twice that night

rows.currentQuote: Go and prepare us the passover, that we may eat.
rows.currentText: Sends Peter and John to prepare the Passover meal

rows.currentQuote: Behold, when ye are entered into the city, there shall a man meet you, bearing a pitcher of water; follow him into the house where he entereth in. And ye shall say unto the goodman of the house, The Master saith unto thee, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples? And he shall shew you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.
rows.currentText: Tells Peter and John they will meet a man carrying a water jar — follow him to the prepared upper room

rows.currentQuote: ¶ But, behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table. And truly the Son of man goeth, as it was determined: but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed!
rows.currentText: Tells the disciples the betrayer's hand is on the table with him — woe to the one who hands the Son of Man over

rows.currentQuote: I tell thee, Peter, the cock shall not crow this day, before that thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me. When I sent you without purse, and scrip, and shoes, lacked ye any thing? But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise his scrip: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one. For I say unto you, that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned among the transgressors: for the things concerning me have an end. It is enough.
rows.currentText: Tells Peter the rooster will not crow before he denies him three times — and warns the disciples to take a purse and sword

rows.currentQuote: All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad. But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.
rows.currentText: Foretells the disciples' scattering, citing Zechariah — but pledges to meet them in Galilee after he rises

rows.currentQuote: Verily I say unto thee, That this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.
rows.currentText: Foretells Peter's three denials before the rooster crows that very night

rows.currentQuote: Friend, wherefore art thou come?
rows.currentText: Greets Judas at the betrayal — friend, do what you came for

rows.currentQuote: Put up again thy sword into his place: for all they that take the sword shall perish with the sword. Thinkest thou that I cannot now pray to my Father, and he shall presently give me more than twelve legions of angels? But how then shall the scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must be? Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves for to take me? I sat daily with you teaching in the temple, and ye laid no hold on me. But all this was done, that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled.
rows.currentText: Stops Peter's sword at his arrest — those who live by the sword die by it; the Scriptures must be fulfilled

rows.currentQuote: ¶ I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me. Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am he. Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me; and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me. Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
rows.currentText: Cites Psalm 41 — one who shares his bread will lift his heel against him; he warns ahead so they will believe

rows.currentQuote: He it is, to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped it. That thou doest, do quickly.
rows.currentText: He identifies the betrayer as the one to whom he gives the dipped sop, then tells Judas, what you do, do quickly.

rows.currentQuote: Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice.
rows.currentText: He questions Peter's claim to lay down his life and foretells that the cock shall not crow until Peter has denied him three times.

rows.currentQuote: Thou sayest.
rows.currentText: Affirms before Pilate that he is the King of the Jews

rows.currentQuote: Are ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and with staves to take me? I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and ye took me not: but the scriptures must be fulfilled.
rows.currentText: Confronts the arresting party — they come with swords as for a robber, though he taught openly daily in the temple

rows.currentQuote: Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.
rows.currentText: Foretells Peter's denial before the rooster crows twice — he later remembers and weeps bitterly

rows.currentQuote: Thou sayest it.
rows.currentText: Affirms before Pilate (Mark) that he is the King of the Jews

rows.currentQuote: Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?
rows.currentText: Confronts Judas at his arrest — does he betray the Son of Man with a kiss?

rows.currentQuote: Suffer ye thus far. Be ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and staves? When I was daily with you in the temple, ye stretched forth no hands against me: but this is your hour, and the power of darkness.
rows.currentText: At his arrest tells the captors no more violence — this is their hour and the power of darkness

rows.currentQuote: Thou sayest it.
rows.currentText: Affirms before Pilate (Luke) that he is the King of the Jews

rows.currentQuote: Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children. For, behold, the days are coming, in the which they shall say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bare, and the paps which never gave suck. Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us. For if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in the dry?
rows.currentText: Tells the weeping women on the way to the cross to weep for themselves and their children, not for him

rows.currentQuote: Whom seek ye?
rows.currentText: Going forth to those who came to arrest him, he asks, Whom do you seek?

rows.currentQuote: Put up thy sword into the sheath: the cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?
rows.currentText: He commands Peter to put up his sword — shall he not drink the cup the Father has given him?

rows.currentQuote: I spake openly to the world; I ever taught in the synagogue, and in the temple, whither the Jews always resort; and in secret have I said nothing. Why askest thou me? ask them which heard me, what I have said unto them: behold, they know what I said.
rows.currentText: Tells the high priest he taught openly in synagogue and temple — those who heard him should be questioned

rows.currentQuote: If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?
rows.currentText: If he has spoken evil, let the officer bear witness of the evil; but if he has spoken well, why is he struck?

rows.currentQuote: Sayest thou this thing of thyself, or did others tell it thee of me?
rows.currentText: He asks Pilate whether he says this of himself, or whether others have told him concerning Jesus.

rows.currentQuote: Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater sin.
rows.currentText: Pilate would have no power against him at all unless it were given from above; therefore the one who delivered him to Pilate has the greater sin.

gapFillers.note: Decision 4: institution-of-Lords-Supper teaching for Matthew (cat-9 stays canonical).
Decision 4: institution words — Luke parallel (covers cup-before, bread, cup-after).
